title: Goan Prawn Balchao

subs.name: squid rings (500 g) — cook for only 3 minutes or they will toughen
subs.notes: squid balchao is equally popular in coastal Goa

subs.name: reduce to 4 chillies and add 2 tsp Kashmiri chilli powder for colour with less heat
tamarind water (3 tbsp pulp in 4 tbsp water)
subs.notes: the taste will be less sharp and more fruity

steps.text: Soak the dried red chillies in 3 tbsp vinegar for 15 minutes. Grind them with garlic, ginger, cumin, cloves, cinnamon and turmeric into a fine, fragrant paste using a blender or stone grinder.
Heat oil in a kadai over medium-high flame. Add the onions and cook until they turn deep golden-brown, about 10 minutes. Stir frequently to prevent burning.
Add the ground spice paste and fry for 2 minutes until the raw smell vanishes and the paste darkens. Add the tomatoes and cook until they break down completely and the oil surfaces, about 5 minutes.
Add the remaining 1 tbsp vinegar and the jaggery. Stir and cook for 1 minute — the masala should be thick, dark and glossy. Season with salt.

Add the prawns and toss to coat them thoroughly in the balchao masala. Cook on high for 4 to 5 minutes, stirring, until the prawns are pink, curled and the sauce has reduced to cling tightly around each prawn.

Taste for the balance of hot, sour and sweet — adjust with more vinegar or jaggery as needed. Balchao can be eaten immediately or stored covered for 2 days in the refrigerator, where it develops a deeper pickle-like intensity.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Cook seafood until safely done for the species and preparation; keep it chilled before cooking.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: prawn → squid rings (500 g) — cook for only 3 minutes or they will toughen (squid balchao is equally popular in coastal Goa); vinegar → tamarind water (3 tbsp pulp in 4 tbsp water) (the taste will be less sharp and more fruity); dried-red-chilli → reduce to 4 chillies and add 2 tsp Kashmiri chilli powder for colour with less heat
